path: Problem: Retrieve an intersecting value from a 2D table where the correct row is identified by multiple criteria and the correct column is chosen by a header match
Action: Use INDEX with a multi-criteria MATCH for the row and a header MATCH for the column (array multiplication of boolean tests)

Observation: Multiplying boolean arrays (TRUE/FALSE) coerces to 1/0, so MATCH(1, ...) finds the first row where all criteria are simultaneously met; a second MATCH selects among multiple value columns by matching the desired header/port
Problem: Return a safe result when no matching row/column exists (avoid #N/A)
Action: Wrap the lookup in IFERROR (or IFNA) to substitute a blank or custom message
Observation: Multi-criteria MATCH and header MATCH can fail independently; error handling prevents downstream formulas from breaking
